prompt: We already hold ISO 27001:2022 certification. Does this mean we are NIS2 compliant? What gaps might still exist between our ISO 27001 ISMS and full NIS2 compliance?
assertions: Clearly states that ISO 27001:2022 certification supports NIS2 compliance but does NOT substitute or automatically satisfy NIS2 legal obligations
Identifies Art. 20 governance gap: management body personal liability and mandatory training have no ISO 27001 equivalent
Identifies Art. 23 incident reporting gap: ISO 27001 covers incident management generally but not the specific 24h/72h/1-month NIS2 reporting timelines to CSIRT/competent authority
Identifies Art. 21(2)(j) MFA gap: ISO 27001 A.8.5 covers authentication but does not universally mandate MFA as NIS2 does
Mentions the requirement to register with the national competent authority and monitor ENISA supply chain risk assessments (Art. 26)
